chain_of_thought: Ghidra MCP access to the libs is blocked with 403, so I cannot decompile stream_gets or its callers. I must reason from the symbolic stack and library map only.

The top native frame is stream_gets in libmainstone.so. Name suggests a custom input function similar to gets()/fgets() for an in‑memory stream or file. Process termination named stream_gets implies an internal abort/assert, likely due to an internal bounds or format check failing.

Call sequence: stream_gets -> ?? -> ms_decode_buf_pnm -> MsDecodeBuffer -> Java_com_intsig_nativelib_BCREngine_RecognizeCardData -> fuzz_one_input. ms_decode_buf_pnm strongly suggests a PNM (portable anymap) image decoder working from a memory buffer, using custom IO helpers (stream_gets).

Typical PNM decoders use line-oriented parsing with helper functions like gets; bugs often include buffer overflows or out-of-bounds reads if lengths are not checked. However, the termination reason is not SIGSEGV/heap corruption but a named abort at stream_gets, suggesting the function itself detects an error (e.g., malformed file or overlong line) and terminates, not that memory corruption happened before.

Without decompilation I cannot see whether stream_gets writes past a fixed buffer or whether it enforces max line length. The more plausible explanation in modern shipping code is that it has an internal maximum and aborts if exceeded—an intended defensive DoS on malformed input.

Attacker control: the Java JNI method RecognizeCardData receives card image data (likely a photo or image bytes) from the app. That ultimately feeds MsDecodeBuffer and ms_decode_buf_pnm. Input is clearly attacker‑controlled (user/card image) and can trigger this path. But the observed behaviour is a clean process abort due to failed invariant in stream_gets; there is no evidence of memory safety violation or exploitable condition, only a crash on malformed / oversized input while parsing PNM.

Fuzz harness: fuzz_one_input is clearly passing arbitrary data directly into JNI, more aggressively than any realistic app usage. It might be feeding completely nonsensical or huge buffers that normal app flows or file formats would not allow. So some asserts that fire under dumb fuzzing are not security bugs if they would not be reachable in production without prior validation or if they only yield a controlled abort.

Given only a process termination in stream_gets, not a low-level SEGV from memcpy/heap, plus lack of any allocator/sanitizer frames, and no evidence of out-of-bounds indexes or missing length checks, I treat this as a non-exploitable robustness bug/DoS: malformed or too-long image data causes a decoder abort. This is still a potential local DoS (app crash) on malformed images, but not clearly a memory safety bug. Severity low, confidence moderate because of limited visibility into code. If later decompilation showed unbounded copy into a fixed buffer, this would be upgraded to CWE‑787/120, but I cannot assert that now.
Therefore I classify it as not a confirmed vulnerability, leaning toward harness/format-driven benign abort rather than exploitable memory corruption.
reasons: Crash is a named process termination in stream_gets, not a low-level SIGSEGV/heap corruption tied to memcpy/memmove
Function names (ms_decode_buf_pnm, MsDecodeBuffer) indicate an image decoder aborting on malformed or oversized input, a robustness DoS rather than proven memory safety issue
No allocator/sanitizer frames or direct evidence of out-of-bounds access; fuzz harness likely feeds unrealistic buffers
recommendations: Decompile stream_gets and ms_decode_buf_pnm to verify any line-buffer bounds checks and ensure they cannot overflow on long inputs
Add graceful error handling instead of hard aborts when stream_gets encounters malformed or overlong data so the app can fail the operation without crashing
Add fuzz tests with structured image inputs to confirm that only clearly malformed or extreme data triggers this termination
assumptions: stream_gets terminates via an internal assert or error path rather than crashing due to memory corruption
Application feeds MsDecodeBuffer primarily with legitimate image data in normal use; only fuzzer sends highly malformed buffers
Lack of Ghidra access prevents confirming exact implementation of stream_gets
limitations: 403 errors prevented decompilation of libmainstone.so and libbcr.so, so no direct code inspection of stream_gets or MsDecodeBuffer
No detailed register/heap state or disassembly for the crashing frame
Cannot see Java-side argument construction for RecognizeCardData to judge realistic input ranges
